selected_title: Decentralised Governance: A Review of the Idea and Issues
question: Where does “Decentralised Governance: A Review of the Idea and Issues” support or contradict the causal account advanced by “Decentralization, democratic governance, and civil society in comparative perspective: Africa, Asia, and Latin America”?

evidence_spans.text: Decentralisation is a dominant debate in development. The enormous and growing discussions on Decentralisation are well underway all over the world by prominent scholars and policy makers. Further, Decentralisation is not a new concept, but a well-formulated and structured view of an old idea. The present paper seeks to understand the basic idea of decentralisd governance from the backdrop of democratic theory and contemporary public administration; and concertedly reviews some of the mundane glaring issues facing decentralised governance.

learned_claims: The inspected source addresses Decentralization, Corporate governance through the evidence summarized in the preserved span.
The abstract supplies a direct evidence span of 78 words; interpretation remains bounded to that span.
